source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A concave mirror of radius \( R \) is kept on a horizontal table as shown in the figure. Water (refractive index \( = \mu \)) is poured into it up to a height \( h \). Where should an object be placed, so that its image is formed on itself?

the setup involves spatial geometry of a concave mirror with water filled to height h, which is likely diagram-dependent

finding where an object forms an image on itself likely requires combining at least two relations from the described configuration

multiple-choice options are available, and answer_text matches one listed choice exactly
